product.name: ArWoWear T21 Static Pro Pro
product.titleDescriptor: 79% Cotton 20% Polyester 1% Carbon Twill, Antisatic Fabric, 330 gr/m'2
product.specLine: 330 g/m², 79% Cotton 20% Polyester 1% Carbon, Antistatic, 3/1S Twill Fabric
descriptions: ArWoWear T21 Flame Arc Pro is heavy weight 330 g/m², 79% Cotton 20% Polyester 1% Carbon, vat dyed, antistatic workwear fabric. Due to carbon fibers used on the back side of the fabric it has antistatic certification. (EN 1149-5)

It is suitable to use on electronic industry where static electric formation is a problem. Oil and gas industry platform workers can also use it to prevent static electric related explosions on dangerous area.
79% Cotton 20% Polyester 1% Carbon Twill, Antisatic Fabric, 330 gr/m'2
330 g/m², 79% Cotton 20% Polyester 1% Carbon, Antistatic, 3/1S Twill Fabric
technicalParameters: Fiber | 79% Cotton (CO) 20% Polyester 1% Carbon(CF) | EN ISO 2076
Tensile Strength | Warp>1200N; Weft>1000N | EN ISO 13934-1
Tear Strength | Warp>25N; Weft>20N | EN ISO 13937-2
Dimensional Change | -3% < Length < +3% ; -3% < Width < +3% | EN ISO 5077
Dry Cleaning Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 D01
Hot Press Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 X11
Perspiration Light Fast. | 2-3 | EN ISO 105 B07
Azo Test | No Azo Colorants. | EN ISO 14362-1

evidence: Suitable to use on electronic industry where static electric formation is a problem.
Oil and gas industry platform workers can also use it to prevent static electric related explosions on dangerous area.
acceptedMatches.rationale: Explicit statement of use by oil and gas platform workers to prevent static-electric related explosions directly connects this fabric to the energy sector, beyond just antistatic property alone.

possibleButNotAccepted.reason: Coverall application is generic industrial workwear; no explicit military or uniformed-service use is stated.
Antistatic and heavy twill workwear could be used in industrial metal environments, but no explicit welding/foundry/metallurgy use is stated.

evidence: Oil and gas industry platform workers can also use it to prevent static electric related explosions on dangerous area.
Heavy weight 330 g/m² antistatic workwear fabric
primary.rationale: The product is explicitly described as a coverall fabric intended for general industrial and oil-and-gas workwear use, which the taxonomy explicitly lists under workwear-industrial's include criteria. This is the dominant, most direct commercial use even though the antistatic property has additional context.

evidence: Suitable to use on electronic industry where static electric formation is a problem.
Oil and gas industry platform workers can also use it to prevent static electric related explosions on dangerous area.
secondary.rationale: The explicit mention of oil-and-gas platform and electronic-industry static-explosion prevention independently supports an energy-sector use beyond the antistatic property alone, satisfying the requirement for explicit connection to electrical/electronic hazardous environments.

alternativesConsidered.reasonNotSelected: No explicit welding, foundry or metal-splash use stated; only generic antistatic workwear context.
No explicit military, police or uniformed-service application stated.

modelOutput.reviewReason: Ambiguity between workwear-industrial and energy sector classification since the fabric's primary application field says 'Coveralls' (generic workwear) but description explicitly cites oil-and-gas and electronic-industry static-explosion prevention, which could be interpreted as either sector depending on taxonomy strictness.
